D0101.en: The Senate chooses Pertinax against his will to succeed Commodus as Roman emperor.[4]

Saint Telemachus tries to stop a gladiatorial fight in a Roman amphitheatre, and is stoned to death by the crowd. This act impresses the Christian Emperor Honorius, who issues a historic ban on gladiatorial fights.[5]
Emperor Honorius forces Galla Placidia into marriage to Constantius, his famous general (magister militum) (probable).[6]
Emperor Taizong of the Khitan-led Liao dynasty captures Daliang, ending the dynasty and empire of the Later Jin.[7]
Grand Prince Stephen I of Hungary is named the first King of Hungary by Pope Sylvester II (probable).[8]
Romanos IV Diogenes marries Eudokia Makrembolitissa and is crowned Byzantine Emperor.[9]
Michael VIII Palaiologos is proclaimed co-emperor of the Empire of Nicaea with his ward John IV Laskaris.[10]
Albert II of Habsburg is crowned King of Hungary.[11]
The present-day location of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, is first explored by the Portuguese.[12]
Twenty-year-old Francis, Duke of Brittany, succeeds to the French throne following the death of his father-in-law, Louis XII.[13]
Croatian nobles elect Ferdinand I, Archduke of Austria as King of Croatia in the 1527 election in Cetin.[14]
Scotland recognises January 1 as the start of the year, instead of March 25.[15]
The Masque of Indian and China Knights is performed by courtiers of James VI and I at Hampton Court.[16]

enAnchors.text: The Masque of Indian and China Knights

D0101.en: Charles II is crowned King of Scotland at Scone Palace.[17]
Russia begins using the Anno Domini era instead of the Anno Mundi era of the Byzantine Empire.[18]
John V is proclaimed King of Portugal and the Algarves in Lisbon.[19]
J. S. Bach leads the first performance of his chorale cantata Jesu, nun sei gepreiset, BWV 41, which features the trumpet fanfares from the beginning also in the end.[20][21]
Bouvet Island, the world's remotest island,[22] is discovered by French explorer Jean-Baptiste Charles Bouvet de Lozier.[23]
The first traveler's cheques, which could be used in 90 European cities, are issued by the London Credit Exchange Company.[24]

The hymn that becomes known as "Amazing Grace", previously titled "1 Chronicles 17:16–17, Faith's Review and Expectation", is first used to accompany a sermon led by John Newton in the town of Olney, Buckinghamshire, England.[25]
American Revolutionary War: Burning of Norfolk – Norfolk, Virginia, is burned to the ground by combined Royal Navy and Continental Army action.[26]
General George Washington hoists the first United States flag, the Continental Union Flag, at Prospect Hill.[27]

American Revolutionary War: One thousand five hundred soldiers of the 6th Pennsylvania Regiment under General Anthony Wayne's command rebel against the Continental Army's winter camp in Morristown, New Jersey in the Pennsylvania Line Mutiny of 1781.[28]
The first edition of The Times of London, previously The Daily Universal Register, is published.[29]
The legislative union of Kingdom of Great Britain and Kingdom of Ireland is completed, and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland is proclaimed.[30]
Ceres, the largest and first known object in the Asteroid belt, is discovered by Giuseppe Piazzi.[31]
French rule ends in Haiti. Haiti becomes the first black-majority republic and second independent country in North America after the United States.[32]
The French Republican Calendar is abolished.[33]
The United States bans the importation of slaves.[34]
Major-General Lachlan Macquarie officially becomes Governor of New South Wales.[35]
Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley (anonymously) publishes the pioneering work of science fiction, Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus, in London.[36]
The Greek Constitution of 1822 is adopted by the First National Assembly at Epidaurus.[37]
Most of Germany forms the Zollverein customs union, the first such union between sovereign states.[38]

The Philippines moves its national calendar to align with other Asian countries' calendars by skipping Tuesday, December 31, 1844. The change has been ordered by Governor–General Narciso Claveria to reform the country's calendar so that it aligns with the rest of Asia. Its territory has been one day behind the rest of Asia for 323 years since the arrival of Ferdinand Magellan in the Philippines on March 16, 1521.[39]
The world's first "Mercy" Hospital is founded in Pittsburgh, United States, by a group of Sisters of Mercy from Ireland;[40] the name will go on to grace over 30 major hospitals throughout the world.
The first Polish postage stamp is issued, replacing the Russian stamps previously in use.[41]

enAnchors.text: Postage stamps and postal history of Poland
Postage stamps and postal history of Russia

D0101.en: Liberal forces supporting Benito Juárez enter Mexico City.[42]
American Civil War: The Emancipation Proclamation takes effect in Confederate territory.[43]
Queen Victoria of the United Kingdom is proclaimed Empress of India.[44]
Twenty-five nations adopt Sandford Fleming's proposal for standard time (and also, time zones).[45]
Eritrea is consolidated into a colony by the Italian government.[46]
Ellis Island begins processing immigrants into the United States.[47]

New York, New York annexes land from surrounding counties, creating the City of Greater New York. The four initial boroughs, Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, and The Bronx, are joined on January 25 by Staten Island to create the modern city of five boroughs.
Spanish rule ends in Cuba.[48]
Nigeria becomes a British protectorate with Frederick Lugard as high commissioner.[49]
The Southern Nigeria Protectorate is established within the British Empire.[50]

The British colonies of New South Wales, Queensland, Victoria, South Australia, Tasmania, and Western Australia federate as the Commonwealth of Australia; Edmund Barton is appointed the first Prime Minister.[51]
The first American college football bowl game, the Rose Bowl between Michigan and Stanford, is held in Pasadena, California.[52]
Captain David Beatty is promoted to rear admiral, and becomes the youngest admiral in the Royal Navy (except for royal family members) since Horatio Nelson.[53]
The Republic of China is established.[54]
The SPT Airboat Line becomes the world's first scheduled airline to use a winged aircraft.[55]
Britain's Railways are grouped into the Big Four: LNER, GWR, SR, and LMS.[56]

enAnchors.text: List of railway companies involved in the 1923 grouping

D0101.en: New Mexican oil legislation goes into effect, leading to the formal outbreak of the Cristero War.[57]
Boris Bazhanov defects through Iran to seek asylum in France. He is the only member of Joseph Stalin's secretariat to have defected from the Soviet Union.[58]
The former municipalities of Point Grey, British Columbia and South Vancouver, British Columbia are amalgamated into Vancouver.[59]
The United States Post Office Department issues a set of 12 stamps commemorating the 200th anniversary of George Washington's birth.[60]
Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay becomes a United States federal prison.[61]
A "Law for the Prevention of Genetically Diseased Offspring" comes into effect in Nazi Germany.[62]
The Declaration by United Nations is signed by twenty-six nations.[63]
World War II: The German Luftwaffe launches Operation Bodenplatte, a massive, but failed, attempt to knock out Allied air power in northern Europe in a single blow.[64]
Cold War: The American and British occupation zones in Allied-occupied Germany, after World War II, merge to form the Bizone, which later (with the French zone) became part of West Germany.[65]
The Canadian Citizenship Act 1946 comes into effect, converting British subjects into Canadian citizens.[66] Prime Minister William Lyon Mackenzie King becomes the first Canadian citizen.
The British railway network is nationalized to form British Railways.[67]
United Nations cease-fire takes effect in Kashmir from one minute before midnight. War between India and Pakistan stops accordingly.[68]
Sudan achieves independence from Egypt and the United Kingdom.[69]
George Town, Penang, is made a city by a royal charter of Queen Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom.[70]
Lèse majesté in Thailand is strengthened to include "insult" and changed to a crime against national security, after the Thai criminal code of 1956 went into effect.[71]: 6, 18
The European Economic Community is established.[72]
Cuban Revolution: Fulgencio Batista, dictator of Cuba, is overthrown by Fidel Castro's forces.[73]
Cameroon achieves independence from France and the United Kingdom.[74]
Western Samoa achieves independence from New Zealand; its name is changed to the Independent State of Western Samoa.[75]
The Federation of Rhodesia and Nyasaland is divided into the independent republics of Zambia and Malawi, and the British-controlled Rhodesia.[76]
The People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan is founded in Kabul, Afghanistan.[77]
The defined beginning of Unix time, at 00:00:00.[78]
Cigarette advertisements are banned on American television.[79]
Denmark, Ireland and the United Kingdom are admitted into the European Economic Community.[80]
A bomb explodes on board Middle East Airlines Flight 438 over Qaisumah, Saudi Arabia, killing all 81 people on board.[81]

Air India Flight 855, a Boeing 747, crashes into the Arabian Sea off the coast of Bombay, India, due to instrument failure, spatial disorientation, and pilot error, killing all 213 people on board.[82]

the Joint Communiqué on the Establishment of Diplomatic Relations and Taiwan Relations Act enter into force. Through the Communiqué, the United States establishes normal diplomatic relations with China.[83] Through the Act, the United States guarantees military support for Taiwan.[84]

enAnchors.text: Joint Communiqué on the Establishment of Diplomatic Relations

D0101.en: Greece is admitted into the European Community.[85]
Peruvian Javier Pérez de Cuéllar becomes the first Latin American to hold the title of Secretary-General of the United Nations.[86]
The ARPANET officially changes to using TCP/IP, the Internet Protocol, effectively creating the Internet.[87]

The original American Telephone & Telegraph Company is divested of its 22 Bell System companies as a result of the settlement of the 1974 United States Department of Justice antitrust suit against AT&T.[88]
Brunei becomes independent of the United Kingdom.[89]
The first British mobile phone call is made by Michael Harrison to his father Sir Ernest Harrison, chairman of Vodafone.[90]
Eastern Air Lines Flight 980 crashes into Mount Illimani in Bolivia, killing all 29 aboard.[91]
The Isleta Pueblo tribe elect Verna Williamson to be their first female governor.[92]
The Evangelical Lutheran Church in America comes into existence, creating the largest Lutheran denomination in the United States.[93]
The Montreal Protocol comes into force, stopping the use of chemicals contributing to ozone depletion.[94]
David Dinkins is sworn in as New York City's first black mayor.[95]
Dissolution of Czechoslovakia: Czechoslovakia is divided into the Czech Republic and Slovak Republic.[96]
The Zapatista Army of National Liberation initiates twelve days of armed conflict in the Mexican state of Chiapas.[97]
The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) comes into effect.[98]
The World Trade Organization comes into being.[99]
The Draupner wave in the North Sea in Norway is detected, confirming the existence of freak waves.[100]
Austria, Finland and Sweden join the EU.[101]
Following a currency reform, Russia begins to circulate new rubles to stem inflation and promote confidence.[102]
Argentinian physicist Juan Maldacena publishes a landmark paper initiating the study of AdS/CFT correspondence, which links string theory and quantum gravity.[103]
The Euro currency is introduced in 11 member nations of the European Union (with the exception of the United Kingdom, Denmark, Greece and Sweden).[104]
Greece adopts the Euro, becoming the 12th Eurozone country.[104]

In a vote of confidence, General Pervez Musharraf wins 658 out of 1,170 votes in the Electoral College of Pakistan, and according to Article 41(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan, is "deemed to be elected" to the office of President until October 2007.[105]
Bulgaria and Romania join the EU.[106]
Adam Air Flight 574 breaks apart in mid-air and crashes near the Makassar Strait, Indonesia, killing all 102 people on board.[107]
Sixty-six people die in a nightclub fire in Bangkok, Thailand.[108]
A suicide car bomber detonates at a volleyball tournament in Lakki Marwat, Pakistan, killing 105 and injuring 100 more.[109]
A bomb explodes as Coptic Christians in Alexandria, Egypt, leave a new year service, killing 23 people.[110]
Estonia officially adopts the Euro currency and becomes the 17th Eurozone country.[111]
At least 60 people are killed and 200 injured in a stampede after celebrations at Félix Houphouët-Boigny Stadium in Abidjan, Ivory Coast.[112]
The Eurasian Economic Union comes into effect, creating a political and economic union between Russia, Belarus, Armenia, Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan.[113]
An attack on a nightclub in Istanbul, Turkey, during New Year's celebrations, kills 39 people and injures 79 others.[114]
Croatia officially adopts the Euro, becoming the 20th Eurozone country, and becomes the 27th member of the Schengen Area.[115]
A 7.5 Mww  earthquake strikes the western coast of Japan, killing more than 500 people and injuring over 1,000 others.[116] A majority of direct deaths were due to collapsed homes.[117]
Disney's copyright protection on Steamboat Willie and the original Mickey Mouse expires as they enter the public domain.[118]
Artsakh ceases to exist.[119]
14 people are killed and 35 others injured during a vehicle-ramming and shooting attack in New Orleans, Louisiana.[120]

D0102.en: The Roman legions in Germania Superior refuse to swear loyalty to Galba. They rebel and proclaim Vitellius as emperor.[1]
The Alemanni cross the frozen Rhine in large numbers, invading the Roman Empire.[2]
Mercurius becomes Pope John II, the first pope to adopt a new name upon elevation to the papacy.[3]
Reconquista: The Emirate of Granada, the last Moorish stronghold in Spain, surrenders.[4]
Trunajaya rebellion: Amangkurat II of Mataram and his bodyguards execute the rebel leader Trunajaya.[5]
Empress Maria Theresa of Austria amends the Constitutio Criminalis Theresiana to include the abolition of torture throughout the Habsburg-ruled countries of Austria and Bohemia.[6]

American Revolutionary War: American forces under the command of General George Washington repulse a British attack led by General Charles Cornwallis at the Battle of the Assunpink Creek near Trenton, New Jersey.[7]
Georgia becomes the fourth state to ratify the United States Constitution.[8]
Northwest Indian War: The Big Bottom massacre is committed by Lenape and Wyandot warriors in the Ohio Country, North America.[9]
The British Institution of Civil Engineers is founded by a group of six engineers; Thomas Telford would later become its first president.[10]

American Civil War: The three-day Battle of Stones River ends in a Union victory when the Confederate Army of Tennessee under General Braxton Bragg are repulsed for the final time by the Army of the Cumberland under General William S. Rosecrans.[11]
Uruguayan War: The Siege of Paysandú ends as the Brazilians and Coloradans capture Paysandú, Uruguay.[12]
American statesman and diplomat John Hay announces the Open Door Policy to promote trade with China.[13]
Chicago Canal opens.[14]
The second Palmer Raid, ordered by the US Department of Justice, results in 6,000 suspected communists and anarchists being arrested and held without trial.[15]
World premiere of the science fiction play R.U.R. by the Czech writer Karel Čapek in a theater in Hradec Králové.[16]
World War II: The Cardiff Blitz severely damages the cathedral in Cardiff, Wales.[17]

The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) obtains the conviction of 33 members of a German spy ring headed by Fritz Joubert Duquesne in the largest espionage case in United States history; Also known as the Duquesne Spy Ring.[18]
World War II: Manila is captured by Japanese forces, enabling them to control the Philippines.[19]
Luis Muñoz Marín is inaugurated as the first democratically elected Governor of Puerto Rico.[20]
India establishes its highest civilian awards, the Bharat Ratna and the Padma Vibhushan.[21]

Following the assassination of the Panamanian president José Antonio Remón Cantera, his deputy, José Ramón Guizado, takes power, but is quickly deposed after his involvement in Cantera's death is discovered.[22]
Luna 1, the first spacecraft to reach the vicinity of the Moon and to orbit the Sun, is launched by the Soviet Union.[23]
Vietnam War: The Viet Cong wins its first major victory, at the Battle of Ap Bac.[24]
Ronald Reagan, past movie actor and future President of the United States, is sworn in as Governor of California.[25]
The second Ibrox disaster kills 66 fans at a Rangers-Celtic association football match.[26]
United States President Richard Nixon signs a bill lowering the maximum U.S. speed limit to 55 mph in order to conserve gasoline during an OPEC embargo.[27]
At the opening of a new railway line, a bomb blast at Samastipur, Bihar, India, fatally wounds Lalit Narayan Mishra, Minister of Railways.[28]
The Federal Rules of Evidence are approved by the United States Congress.[29]

The Gale of January 1976 begins, resulting in coastal flooding around the southern North Sea coasts, affecting countries from Ireland to Yugoslavia and causing at least 82 deaths and US$1.3 billion in damage.[30]

On the orders of the President of Pakistan, Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq, paramilitary forces opened fire on peaceful protesting workers in Multan, Pakistan; it is known as 1978 massacre at Multan Colony Textile Mills.[31]
One of the largest investigations by a British police force ends when serial killer Peter Sutcliffe, the "Yorkshire Ripper", is arrested in Sheffield, South Yorkshire.[32]
Condor Flugdienst Flight 3782 crashes near Seferihisar, Turkey, killing 16 people.[33]
Sharon Pratt Dixon becomes the first African American woman mayor of a major city and first woman Mayor of the District of Columbia.[34]
Sri Lankan Civil War: The Sri Lanka Navy kill 35–100 civilians on the Jaffna Lagoon.[35]
Stardust successfully flies past Comet Wild 2, collecting samples that are returned to Earth.[36]
Massive nationwide protests and unrest break out in Kazakhstan over the sudden increase of liquefied petroleum gas prices, leading to 238 people dead and thousands injured by January 11.[37]
Two aircraft collide on a runway at Haneda Airport in Tokyo, killing 5 and injuring 18.[38]

D0103.en: The Roman legions on the Rhine refuse to declare their allegiance to Galba, instead proclaiming their legate, Aulus Vitellius, as emperor.[1]
Emperor Decius orders everyone in the Roman Empire (except Jews) to make sacrifices to the Roman gods.[2]
Pope Leo X excommunicates Martin Luther in the papal bull Decet Romanum Pontificem.[3]
By the Coonan Cross Oath, the Eastern Church in India cuts itself off from colonial Portuguese tutelage.[4]
Benning Wentworth issues the first of the New Hampshire Grants, leading to the establishment of Vermont.[5]
The first issue of Berlingske, Denmark's oldest continually operating newspaper, is published.[6]
American Revolutionary War: American forces under General George Washington defeat British forces at the Battle of Princeton, helping boost patriot morale.[7]
Austria, the United Kingdom, and France form a secret defensive alliance against Prussia and Russia.[8]
Captain James Onslow, in the Clio, reasserts British sovereignty over the Falkland Islands.[9]

enAnchors.text: Reassertion of British sovereignty over the Falkland Islands (1833)

D0103.en: Joseph Jenkins Roberts is sworn in as the first president of Liberia.[10]
American Civil War: Delaware votes not to secede from the United States.[11]
Meiji Restoration in Japan: The Tokugawa shogunate is abolished; agents of Satsuma and Chōshū seize power.[12]
Construction work begins on the Brooklyn Bridge in New York, United States.[13]
In the Battle of Bapaume, an engagement in the Franco-Prussian War, General Louis Faidherbe's forces bring about a Prussian retreat.[14]
Sino-French War: Beginning of the Battle of Núi Bop.[15]
A magnitude 7.7 earthquake destroys the city of Almaty in Russian Turkestan.[16]
A gun battle in the East End of London leaves two dead. It sparked a political row over the involvement of then-Home Secretary Winston Churchill.[17]
An Atlantic coast storm sets the lowest confirmed barometric pressure reading (955.0 mb (28.20 inHg)) for a non-tropical system in the continental United States.[18]
First Balkan War: Greece completes its capture of the eastern Aegean island of Chios, as the last Ottoman forces on the island surrender.[19][20]
Over 640 are killed after a magnitude 6.4 earthquake strikes the Mexican states Puebla and Veracruz.[21]
Minnie D. Craig becomes the first woman elected as Speaker of the North Dakota House of Representatives, the first woman to hold a Speaker position anywhere in the United States.[22]

enAnchors.text: Speaker of the North Dakota House of Representatives

D0103.en: World War II: US flying ace Major Greg "Pappy" Boyington is shot down in his Vought F4U Corsair by Captain Masajiro Kawato flying a Mitsubishi A6M Zero.[23]

Popular Canadian American jockey George Woolf suffers a concussion during a freak racing accident; he dies from the injury the following day. The annual George Woolf Memorial Jockey Award is created to honor him.[24]
Proceedings of the U.S. Congress are televised for the first time.[25]
The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas, the central bank of the Philippines, is established.[26]
Frances P. Bolton and her son, Oliver from Ohio, become the first mother and son to serve simultaneously in the U.S. Congress.[27]
A fire damages the top part of the Eiffel Tower.[28]
The Hamilton Watch Company introduces the first electric watch.[29]
The West Indies Federation is formed.[30]
Alaska is admitted as the 49th U.S. state.[31]
Cold War: After a series of economic retaliations against one another, the United States severs diplomatic relations with Cuba.[32]
The SL-1 nuclear reactor, near Idaho Falls, Idaho, is destroyed by a steam explosion in the only reactor incident in the United States to cause immediate fatalities.[33]
A protest by agricultural workers in Baixa de Cassanje, Portuguese Angola, turns into a revolt, opening the Angolan War of Independence, the first of the Portuguese Colonial Wars.[34]
Aero Flight 311 crashes into the forest in Kvevlax, Finland, killing 25 people.[35]
Pope John XXIII excommunicates Fidel Castro.[36]
The International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights, adopted by the United Nations General Assembly, comes into force.[37]

enAnchors.text: International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights

D0103.en: Apple Computer is incorporated.[38]
Varig Flight 797 crashes near Akouré in the Ivory Coast, resulting in 50 deaths.[39]
United States invasion of Panama: Manuel Noriega, former leader of Panama, surrenders to American forces.[40]
CommutAir Flight 4821 crashes on approach to Adirondack Regional Airport, in Saranac Lake, New York, killing two people.[41]
In Moscow, Russia, George H. W. Bush and Boris Yeltsin sign the second Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (START).[42]
Baikal Airlines Flight 130 crashes near Mamoney, Irkutsk, Russia, resulting in 125 deaths.[43]
The Mars Polar Lander is launched by NASA.[44]
Israeli–Palestinian conflict: Israeli forces seize the Palestinian freighter Karine A in the Red Sea, finding 50 tons of weapons.[45]
Flash Airlines Flight 604 crashes into the Red Sea, resulting in 148 deaths, making it one of the deadliest aviation accidents in Egyptian history.[46]
The first block of the blockchain of the decentralized payment system Bitcoin, called the Genesis block, is established by the creator of the system, Satoshi Nakamoto.[47]
Boko Haram militants destroy the entire town of Baga in north-east Nigeria, starting the Baga massacre and killing as many as 2,000 people.[48]
In response to the execution of Nimr al-Nimr, Iran ends its diplomatic relations with Saudi Arabia.[49]
For the first time in history, all five major storm surge gates in the Netherlands are closed simultaneously in the wake of a storm.[50]
Chang'e 4 makes the first soft landing on the far side of the Moon, deploying the Yutu-2 lunar rover.[51]
Iranian General Qasem Soleimani is killed by an American airstrike near Baghdad International Airport, igniting global concerns of a potential armed conflict.[52]
Singapore's Jurong Bird Park permanently closes.[53]
At least 91 people are killed in bombings in Kerman, Iran, during a ceremony commemorating the assassination of Iranian General Qasem Soleimani four years earlier.[54]

D0104.en: Battle of Reading: Æthelred of Wessex and his brother Alfred are defeated by a Danish invasion army.[2]
English Civil War: King Charles I, accompanied by 400 soldiers, attempts to arrest five members of Parliament for treason, only to discover the men had been tipped off and fled.[3]
English Civil War: The Rump Parliament votes to put Charles I on trial.[4]
The Netherlands, Great Britain, and France sign the Triple Alliance.[5]
Great Britain declares war on Spain, which meant the entry of Spain into the Seven Years' War.[6]
Constantine Hangerli arrives in Bucharest, Wallachia, as its new Prince, invested by the Ottoman Empire.[7]
The first issue of the Swedish-languaged Saima newspaper founded by J. V. Snellman is published in Kuopio, Finland.[8][9]
After having been kidnapped and sold into slavery in the American South, Solomon Northup regains his freedom; his memoir Twelve Years a Slave later becomes a national bestseller.[10]
The McDonald Islands are discovered by Captain William McDonald aboard the Samarang.[11]
The New Apostolic Church, a Christian and chiliastic church, is established in Hamburg, Germany.[12]
Russo-Turkish War (1877–78): Sofia is liberated from Ottoman rule.[13]
The Fabian Society is founded in London, United Kingdom.[14]
Sino-French War: French troops under General Oscar de Négrier defeat a numerically superior Qing force at Núi Bop in northern Vietnam.[15]
Utah is admitted as the 45th U.S. state.[16]
Topsy, an elephant, is electrocuted by the owners of Luna Park, Coney Island. The Edison film company records the film Electrocuting an Elephant of Topsy's death.[17]
Explorer Aeneas Mackintosh of the Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition escaped death by fleeing across ice floes.[18]
The Scout Association is incorporated throughout the British Empire by royal charter.[19]
The Finnish Declaration of Independence is recognized by Russia, Sweden, Germany and France.[20]
World War II: Operation Carpetbagger, involving the dropping of arms and supplies to resistance fighters in Europe, begins.[21]
The first day of a three-day "disastrous" tornado outbreak across the south-central United States leaves 41 people dead and at least 412 others injured.[22][23]
Burma gains its independence from the United Kingdom, becoming an independent republic.[24]
Korean War: Chinese and North Korean forces capture Seoul for the second time.[25]
The Greek National Radical Union is formed by Konstantinos Karamanlis.[26]
Sputnik 1, the first artificial Earth satellite, launched by the Soviet Union in 1957, falls to Earth from orbit.[27]
Luna 1 becomes the first spacecraft to reach the vicinity of the Moon.[28]
Aeroflot Flight 101/X-20 crashes on approach to Alma-Ata Airport, killing 64 people.[29]
Rose Heilbron becomes the first female judge to sit at the Old Bailey in London, UK.[30]
This date overflowed the 12-bit field that had been used in TOPS-10. There were numerous problems and crashes related to this bug while an alternative format was developed.[31]

The Troubles: The Ulster Volunteer Force shoots dead six Irish Catholic civilians in County Armagh, Northern Ireland. The next day, gunmen would shoot dead ten Protestant civilians nearby in retaliation.[32]
The Maryland train collision: An Amtrak train en route to Boston from Washington, D.C., collides with Conrail engines in Chase, Maryland, United States, killing 16 people.[33]
Second Gulf of Sidra incident: A pair of Libyan MiG-23 "Floggers" are shot down by a pair of US Navy F-14 Tomcats during an air-to-air confrontation.[34]
In Pakistan's deadliest train accident an overloaded passenger train collides with an empty freight train, resulting in 307 deaths and 700 injuries.[35]
A massive ice storm hits eastern Canada and the northeastern United States, continuing through January 10 and causing widespread destruction.[36]
Former professional wrestler Jesse Ventura is sworn in as governor of Minnesota, United States.[37]
A Norwegian passenger train departing from Trondheim, collides with a local train coming from Hamar in Åsta in Åmot Municipality; 19 people are killed and 68 injured in the accident.[38]
Spirit, a NASA Mars rover, lands successfully on Mars at 04:35 UTC.[39]
Mikheil Saakashvili is elected President of Georgia following the November 2003 Rose Revolution.[40]
Ehud Olmert becomes acting Prime Minister of Israel after the incumbent, Ariel Sharon, suffers a second, apparently more serious stroke.[41]
The 110th United States Congress convenes, electing Nancy Pelosi as the first female Speaker of the House in U.S. history.[42]
A Let L-410 Turbolet crashes in the Los Roques Archipelago in Venezuela, killing 14 people.[43]

enAnchors.text: 2008 Los Roques archipelago Transaven Let L-410 crash

D0104.en: The Burj Khalifa, the current tallest building in the world, officially opens in Dubai.[44]
A gunman kills eight people in a house-to-house rampage in Kawit, Cavite, Philippines.[45]

Hennenman–Kroonstad train crash: A passenger train operated by Shosholoza Meyl collides with a truck on a level crossing at Geneva Station between Hennenman and Kroonstad, Free State, South Africa. Twenty people are killed and 260 injured.[46]
A fire in an escape room in Koszalin, Poland, kills five teenagers through carbon monoxide poisoning.[47]

D0105.en: Battle of Nancy: Charles the Bold is defeated and killed in a conflict with René II, Duke of Lorraine; Burgundy subsequently becomes part of France.[1]
Battle of Colmar: The French army defeats forces from Austria and Brandenburg.[2]

Louis XV of France survives an assassination attempt by Robert-François Damiens, who becomes the last person to be executed in France by drawing and quartering (the traditional form of capital punishment used for regicides).[3]
American Revolutionary War: Richmond, Virginia, is burned by British naval forces led by former American general Benedict Arnold.[4]
The government of Central America votes for total annexation to the First Mexican Empire.[5]
The Palais Garnier, one of the most famous opera houses in the world, is inaugurated in Paris.[6]
Dreyfus affair: French army officer Alfred Dreyfus is stripped of his rank and sentenced to life imprisonment on Devil's Island.[7]
Irish nationalist leader John Edward Redmond calls for revolt against British rule.[8]
Kappa Alpha Psi, the world's third-oldest and largest black fraternity, is founded at Indiana University.[9]

The sixth All-Russian Conference of the Russian Social Democratic Labour Party (Prague Party Conference) opens. In the course of the conference, Vladimir Lenin and his supporters break from the rest of the party to form the Bolshevik movement.[10]

First Balkan War: The Battle of Lemnos begins; Greek admiral Pavlos Kountouriotis forces the Turkish fleet to retreat to its base within the Dardanelles, from which it did not venture for the rest of the war.[11]
The Ford Motor Company announces an eight-hour workday and minimum daily wage of $5 in salary plus bonuses.[12]
The German Workers' Party, which would become the Nazi Party, is founded in Munich.[13]
Nellie Tayloe Ross of Wyoming becomes the first female governor in the United States.[14]
Construction of the Golden Gate Bridge begins in San Francisco Bay.[15]
Amy Johnson, a 37-year-old pilot and the first woman to fly solo from London to Australia, disappears after bailing out of her plane over the River Thames, and is presumed dead.[16]
The Daily Mail becomes the first major London newspaper to be published on both sides of the Atlantic Ocean.[17]
The Soviet Union recognizes the new pro-Soviet Provisional Government of the Republic of Poland.[18]

enAnchors.text: Provisional Government of the Republic of Poland

D0105.en: In his State of the Union address, United States President Harry S. Truman unveils his Fair Deal program.[19]
The play Waiting for Godot by Samuel Beckett receives its première in Paris.[20]
In a speech given to the United States Congress, United States President Dwight D. Eisenhower announces the establishment of what will later be called the Eisenhower Doctrine.[21]
Cultural Revolution: The Shanghai People's Commune is established following the seizure of power from local city officials by revolutionaries.[22]
Alexander Dubček comes to power in Czechoslovakia, effectively beginning the "Prague Spring".[23]
The Venera 5 space probe is launched at 06:28:08 UTC[24] from Baikonur.[25][26]
Ariana Afghan Airlines Flight 701 crashes in Fernhill, West Sussex, UK, while on approach to Gatwick Airport, killing 50 people.[27]

The 7.1 Mw  Tonghai earthquake shakes Tonghai County, Yunnan province, China, with a maximum Mercalli intensity of X (Extreme). Between 10,000 and 15,000 people are known to have been killed and about another 26,000 are injured.[28]
US President Richard Nixon announces the Space Shuttle program.[29]
The Tasman Bridge in Tasmania, Australia, is struck by the bulk ore carrier Lake Illawarra, killing twelve people.[30]
The Khmer Rouge announce that the new Constitution of Democratic Kampuchea is ratified.[31]

The Troubles: Gunmen shoot dead ten Protestant civilians after stopping their minibus at Kingsmill in County Armagh, Northern Ireland, UK, allegedly as retaliation for a string of attacks on Catholic civilians in the area by Loyalists, particularly the killing of six Catholics the night before.[32]
Georgian forces enter Tskhinvali, the capital of South Ossetia, Georgia, opening the 1991–92 South Ossetia War.[33]
Somali Civil War: The United States Embassy to Somalia in Mogadishu is evacuated by helicopter airlift days after the outbreak of violence in Mogadishu.[34]

enAnchors.text: Embassy of the United States, Mogadishu

D0105.en: The oil tanker MV Braer runs aground on the coast of the Shetland Islands, spilling 84,700 tons of crude oil.[35]
The dwarf planet Eris is discovered by Palomar Observatory-based astronomers, later motivating the International Astronomical Union (IAU) to define the term planet for the first time.[36]
A launch of the communication satellite GSAT-14 aboard the GSLV MK.II D5 marks the first successful flight of an Indian cryogenic engine.[37]
Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev dismisses Prime Minister Asqar Mamin and declares state of emergency over the 2022 Kazakh unrest.[38]

Alaska Airlines Flight 1282 makes an emergency landing at Portland International Airport after a door plug blows off the Boeing 737 MAX 9 operating the flight. There are no fatalities, but the accident prompts the 737 MAX to be grounded and renews scrutiny on Boeing's manufacturing and design issues.[39]

D0106.en: Following the death of Edward the Confessor on the previous day, the Witan meets to confirm Harold Godwinson as the new King of England; Harold is crowned the same day, sparking a succession crisis that will eventually lead to the Norman conquest of England.[1]
Philip of Swabia undergoes a second coronation as King of the Romans.[2]
Stephen Uroš III is crowned King of Serbia, having defeated his half-brother Stefan Konstantin in battle. His son is crowned "young king" in the same ceremony.[3]
Charles IV of Bohemia is crowned with the Iron Crown of Lombardy as King of Italy in Milan.[4]
Constantine XI is crowned Byzantine Emperor at Mystras.[5]
The Catholic Monarchs Ferdinand and Isabella enter Granada at the conclusion of the Granada War.[6]
The first European school of higher learning in the Americas, Colegio de Santa Cruz de Tlatelolco, is founded by Viceroy Antonio de Mendoza and Bishop Juan de Zumárraga in Mexico City.[7]

enAnchors.text: Colegio de Santa Cruz de Tlatelolco

D0106.en: King Henry VIII of England marries Anne of Cleves.[8]
The Union of Arras unites the southern Netherlands under the Duke of Parma (Ottavio Farnese), governor in the name of King Philip II of Spain.[9]
Arauco War: The first Parliament of Quillín is celebrated, putting a temporary hold on hostilities between Mapuches and Spanish in Chile.[10]
English Restoration: The Fifth Monarchists unsuccessfully attempt to seize control of London, England. The revolt is suppressed after a few days.[11]
The Committee of Inquiry on the South Sea Bubble publishes its findings, revealing details of fraud among company directors and corrupt politicians.[12]
Sie werden aus Saba alle kommen, BWV 65, a Bach cantata, for Epiphany, is performed the first time.[13]

enAnchors.text: Sie werden aus Saba alle kommen, BWV 65

D0106.en: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Liebster Immanuel, Herzog der Frommen, BWV 123, a chorale cantata for Epiphany.[14]

enAnchors.text: Liebster Immanuel, Herzog der Frommen, BWV 123

D0106.en: In the Battle of Jersey, the British defeat the last attempt by France to invade Jersey in the Channel Islands.[15]
Combined British, Portuguese and colonial Brazilian forces begin the Invasion of Cayenne during the Napoleonic Wars.[16]
Alfred Vail and colleagues demonstrate a telegraph system using dots and dashes (this is the forerunner of Morse code).[17]
The Night of the Big Wind, the most damaging storm in 300 years, sweeps across Ireland, damaging or destroying more than 20% of the houses in Dublin.[18]
Samuel Colt obtains his first contract for the sale of revolver pistols to the United States government.[19]
The inauguration of the Musikverein in Vienna, Austria.[20]
Second Boer War: Having already besieged the fortress at Ladysmith, Boer forces attack it, but are driven back by British defenders.[21]
Maria Montessori opens her first school and daycare center for working class children in Rome, Italy.[22]
New Mexico is admitted to the Union as the 47th U.S. state.[23]
German geophysicist Alfred Wegener first presents his theory of continental drift.[24]
King Alexander of the Serbs, Croats and Slovenes suspends his country's constitution, starting the January 6th Dictatorship.[25]
Mother Teresa arrives by sea in Calcutta, India, to begin her work among India's poorest and sick people.[26]
Clessie Cummins arrives at the National Automobile Show in New York City, having driven a car powered by one of his diesel engines from Indianapolis.[27]
United States President Franklin D. Roosevelt delivers his Four Freedoms speech in the State of the Union address.[28]
The first general election ever in Vietnam is held.[29]
Pan American Airlines becomes the first commercial airline to offer a round-the-world ticket.[30]
The United Kingdom recognizes the People's Republic of China.[31] The Republic of China severs diplomatic relations with the UK in response.
Korean War: Beginning of the Ganghwa massacre, in the course of which an estimated 200–1,300 South Korean communist sympathizers are slaughtered.[32]
National Airlines Flight 2511 is destroyed in mid-air by a bomb, while en route from New York City to Miami.[33]
The Associations Law comes into force in Iraq, allowing registration of political parties.[34]
Vietnam War: United States Marine Corps and ARVN troops launch "Operation Deckhouse Five" in the Mekong River delta.[35]
Aeroflot Flight 1668 crashes near Olyokminsk, killing 45.[36]
Allegheny Airlines Flight 737 crashes in Lafayette Township, McKean County, Pennsylvania, United States, killing 11.[37]
In response to the 1973 oil crisis, daylight saving time commences nearly four months early in the United States.[38]
Aeroflot Flight H-75 crashes near Mukachevo, killing 24.[39]
Satwant Singh and Kehar Singh are sentenced to death for conspiracy in the assassination of Prime Minister Indira Gandhi; the two men are executed the same day.[40]
President of Georgia Zviad Gamsakhurdia flees the country as a result of the military coup.[41]
Indian Border Security Force units kill 55 Kashmiri civilians in Sopore, Jammu and Kashmir, in revenge after militants ambushed a BSF patrol.[42]
Four people are killed when Lufthansa CityLine Flight 5634 crashes on approach to Charles de Gaulle Airport in Roissy-en-France, France.[43]
U.S. figure skater Nancy Kerrigan is attacked and injured by an assailant hired by her rival Tonya Harding's ex-husband during the U.S. Figure Skating Championships.[44]
A chemical fire in an apartment complex in Manila, Philippines, leads to the discovery of plans for Project Bojinka, a mass-terrorist attack.[45]
The last natural Pyrenean ibex, Celia, is killed by a falling tree, thus making the species extinct.[46]
Edgar Ray Killen is indicted for the 1964 murders of Chaney, Goodman, and Schwerner during the American Civil Rights Movement.[47]

enAnchors.text: Murders of Chaney, Goodman, and Schwerner

D0106.en: A train collision in Graniteville, South Carolina, United States, releases about 60 tons of chlorine gas.[48]
Twenty-six people are killed and 63 wounded when a suicide bomber blows himself up at a police station in Damascus.[49]
Five people are killed and six others injured in a mass shooting at Fort Lauderdale–Hollywood International Airport in Broward County, Florida.[50]
Forty people are killed in a gold mine collapse in Badakhshan province, in northern Afghanistan.[51]
Muhammad V of Kelantan resigns as the Yang di-Pertuan Agong of Malaysia, becoming the first monarch to do so.[52]
Americans storm the United States Capitol Building to disrupt certification of the 2020 presidential election, resulting in four deaths and evacuation of the U.S. Congress.[53]

enAnchors.text: Attempts to overturn the 2020 United States presidential election

D0106.en: Justin Trudeau announces his resignation as leader of the Liberal Party of Canada and Prime Minister of Canada after nine years in office.[54][55][56]

D0107.en: Afonso IV becomes King of Portugal.[2]
French troops, led by Francis, Duke of Guise, take Calais, the last continental possession of England.[3]
Fire destroys Jamestown, Virginia.[4]
Galileo Galilei makes his first observation of the four Galilean moons: Ganymede, Callisto, Io and Europa, although he is not able to distinguish the last two until the following night.[5]
Battle of Zlatoust: Battle between Bashkir and Tatar rebels and the government troops of the Tsardom of Russia. It is one of the events of the Bashkir rebellion of 1704–1711.[6]
Bashkir rebels besiege Yelabuga.[7]
A peace treaty is signed between Peshwa Bajirao and Jai Singh II following Maratha victory in the Battle of Bhopal.[8]
The first American commercial bank, the Bank of North America, opens.[9]
Frenchman Jean-Pierre Blanchard and American John Jeffries travel from Dover, England, to Calais, France, in a gas balloon.[10]
HMS Beagle, with Charles Darwin on board, drops anchor off the Chonos Archipelago.[11]
The Kingstree jail fire kills 22 freedmen in Reconstruction-era South Carolina.[12]
Thomas Edison makes a kinetoscopic film of someone sneezing.[13] On the same day, his employee, William Kennedy Dickson, receives a patent for motion picture film.[14]
The distress signal "CQD" is established only to be replaced two years later by "SOS".[15]
Montenegrin guerrilla fighters rebel against the planned annexation of Montenegro by Serbia, but fail.[16]
The New York State Assembly refuses to seat five duly elected Socialist assemblymen.[17]
Dáil Éireann ratifies the Anglo-Irish Treaty by a 64–57 vote.[citation needed]
The first transatlantic commercial telephone service is established from New York City to London.[18]
A disastrous flood of the River Thames kills 14 people and causes extensive damage to much of riverside London.[19]
Guy Menzies flies the first solo non-stop trans-Tasman flight (from Australia to New Zealand) in 11 hours and 45 minutes, crash-landing on New Zealand's west coast.[20]
Benito Mussolini and French Foreign Minister Pierre Laval sign the Franco-Italian Agreement.[21]
Winter War: Battle of Raate Road: The Finnish 9th Division finally defeat the numerically superior Soviet forces on the Raate-Suomussalmi road.[22]
Kentucky Air National Guard pilot Thomas Mantell crashes while in pursuit of a supposed UFO.[23]

In the Sverdlovsk air disaster, all 19 of those on board are killed, including almost the entire national ice hockey team (VVS Moscow) of the Soviet Air Force – 11 players, as well as a team doctor and a masseur.[24]
Georgetown–IBM experiment: The first public demonstration of a machine translation system is held in New York at the head office of IBM.[25]
Contralto Marian Anderson becomes the first person of color to perform at the Metropolitan Opera in Giuseppe Verdi's Un ballo in maschera.[26]
The United States recognizes the new Cuban government of Fidel Castro.[27]
Surveyor program: Surveyor 7, the last spacecraft in the Surveyor series, lifts off from Cape Canaveral Launch Complex 36A.[28]
Iberia Flight 602 crashes near Ibiza Airport, killing all 104 people on board.[29]
In his second shooting spree of the week, Mark Essex fatally shoots seven people and wounds five others at Howard Johnson's Hotel in New Orleans, before being shot to death by police officers.[30]
Third Indochina War: Cambodian–Vietnamese War: Phnom Penh falls to the advancing Vietnamese troops, driving out Pol Pot and the Khmer Rouge.[31]
U.S. President Jimmy Carter authorizes legislation giving $1.5 billion in loans to bail out the Chrysler Corporation.[32]
Brunei becomes the sixth member of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN).[33]

Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency launches Sakigake, Japan's first interplanetary spacecraft and the first deep space probe to be launched by any country other than the United States or the Soviet Union.[34]
Sutton United, a team in the fifth tier of English league football, defeated top-tier Coventry City in one of the biggest upsets in FA Cup history.[35]
Roger Lafontant, former leader of the Tonton Macoute in Haiti under François Duvalier, attempts a coup d'état, which ends in his arrest.[36]
The Fourth Republic of Ghana is inaugurated with Jerry Rawlings as president.[37]
Bosnian War: The Bosnian Army executes a surprise attack at the village of Kravica in Srebrenica.[38]

enAnchors.text: Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina

D0107.en: A British Aerospace Jetstream 41 operating as United Express Flight 6291 crashes in Gahanna, Ohio, killing five of the eight people on board.[39]
The Senate trial in the impeachment of U.S. President Bill Clinton begins.[40]
A hot air balloon crashes near Carterton, New Zealand, killing all 11 people on board.[41]
Two gunmen commit mass murder at the offices of Charlie Hebdo in Paris, executing twelve people and wounding eleven others.[42]
A car bomb explodes outside a police college in the Yemeni capital Sana'a with at least 38 people reported dead and more than 63 injured.[43]
The 6.4Mw  2019–20 Puerto Rico earthquakes kill four and injure nine in southern Puerto Rico.[44]

The longest U.S. House of Representatives speaker election since the December 1859 – February 1860 U.S. speaker election concludes and Kevin McCarthy is elected 55th Speaker of the United States House of Representatives.[45][46]

enAnchors.text: January 2023 Speaker of the United States House of Representatives election

D0107.en: A series of wildfires ravage the Greater Los Angeles area, resulting in at least 16 deaths and 13,401 structures destroyed.[47]

D0108.en: Sima Chi becomes emperor of the Jin dynasty in succession to his brother, Sima Zhong, despite a challenge from his other brother, Sima Ying.[1]
Æthelred I and Alfred the Great lead a West Saxon army to repel an invasion by Danelaw Vikings.[2]
François Grimaldi, disguised as a monk, leads his men to capture the fortress protecting the Rock of Monaco, establishing his family as the rulers of Monaco.[3]
The papal bull Romanus Pontifex awards the Kingdom of Portugal exclusive trade and colonization rights to all of Africa south of Cape Bojador.[4]
Louis XII of France marries Anne of Brittany in accordance with a law set by his predecessor, Charles VIII.[5]
The first Lithuanian-language book, the Catechism of Martynas Mažvydas, is published in Königsberg.[6]
The premiere of George Frideric Handel's Ariodante takes place at the Royal Opera House, Covent Garden.[7]
Second Jacobite rising: Bonnie Prince Charlie occupies Stirling.[8]
George Washington delivers the first State of the Union address in New York City.[9]
The Dutch Cape Colony in southern Africa becomes the British Cape Colony as a result of the Battle of Blaauwberg.[10]
Charles Deslondes leads an unsuccessful slave revolt in the North American settlements of St. Charles and St. James, Louisiana.[11]
War of 1812: Battle of New Orleans: Andrew Jackson leads American forces in victory over the British.[12]
The Democratic Party of the United States is organized.[13]
US President Andrew Jackson announces a celebratory dinner after having reduced the United States national debt to zero for the only time.[14]

enAnchors.text: National debt of the United States

D0108.en: American Civil War: Second Battle of Springfield.[15]
The United States Congress passes the bill to allow African American men the right to vote in Washington, D.C.[16]
Crazy Horse and his warriors fight their last battle against the United States Cavalry at Wolf Mountain, Montana Territory.[17]
Herman Hollerith is issued US patent #395,791 for the 'Art of Applying Statistics' — his punched card calculator.[18]
President William McKinley  places Alaska under military rule.[19]
The African National Congress is founded, under the name South African Native National Congress (SANNC).[20]
U.S. President Woodrow Wilson announces his "Fourteen Points" as conditions for ending World War I.[21]
The steel strike of 1919 ends in failure for the Amalgamated Association of Iron, Steel and Tin Workers labor union.[22]

enAnchors.text: Amalgamated Association of Iron and Steel Workers

D0108.en: Crown Prince Nguyễn Phúc Vĩnh Thuỵ is crowned emperor of Vietnam, the country's last monarch.[23]
Abdul-Aziz ibn Saud is crowned King of Hejaz.[24]
Anarchist insurrection of January 1933 breaks out in Barcelona, Spain.[25]
Kashf-e hijab decree is made and immediately enforced by Reza Shah, Iran's head of state, banning the wearing of Islamic veils in public.[26]
World War II: Britain introduces food rationing.[27]
World War II: Philippine Commonwealth troops under the Philippine Commonwealth Army units enter the province of Ilocos Sur in Northern Luzon and attack invading Japanese Imperial forces.[28]

Andrei Zhdanov, Chairman of the Finnish Allied Commission, submitted to the Finnish War Criminal Court an interrogation report by General Erich Buschenhagen, a German prisoner of war, on the contacts between Finnish and German military personnel before the Continuation War and a copy of Hitler's Barbarossa plan.[29]
Operation Auca: Five U.S. missionaries are killed by the Waorani of Ecuador shortly after making first contact.[30]
Charles de Gaulle is proclaimed as the first President of the French Fifth Republic.[31]
In France a referendum supports Charles de Gaulle's policies in Algeria.[32]
President Lyndon B. Johnson declares a "War on Poverty" in the United States.[33]

Bowing to international pressure, President of Pakistan Zulfikar Ali Bhutto releases Bengali leader Sheikh Mujibur Rahman from prison, who had been arrested after declaring the independence of Bangladesh.[34]
Soviet space mission Luna 21 is launched.[35]
Watergate scandal: The trial of seven men accused of illegal entry into Democratic Party headquarters at Watergate begins.[36]
Ella T. Grasso becomes Governor of Connecticut, the first woman to serve as a Governor in the United States other than by succeeding her husband.[37]
Three bombs explode in Moscow, Russia, Soviet Union, within 37 minutes, killing seven. The bombings are attributed to an Armenian separatist group.[38]
A local farmer reports a UFO sighting in Trans-en-Provence, France, claimed to be "perhaps the most completely and carefully documented sighting of all time".[39]
Breakup of the Bell System: In the United States, AT&T agrees to divest itself of twenty-two subdivisions.[37]
Kegworth air disaster: British Midland Flight 92, a Boeing 737-400, crashes into the M1 motorway, killing 47 of the 126 people on board.[40]
Russian cosmonaut Valeri Polyakov on Soyuz TM-18 leaves for Mir. He would stay on the space station until March 22, 1995, for a record 437 days in space.[41]
An Antonov An-32 cargo aircraft crashes into a crowded market in Kinshasa, Zaire, killing up to 223 people on the ground; two of six crew members are also killed.[42]
President of the United States George W. Bush signs into law the No Child Left Behind Act.[43]
Turkish Airlines Flight 634 crashes near Diyarbakır Airport, Turkey, killing the entire crew and 70 of the 75 passengers.[44]
Air Midwest Flight 5481 crashes at Charlotte-Douglas Airport, in Charlotte, North Carolina, killing all 21 people on board.[45]
The RMS Queen Mary 2, then the largest ocean liner ever built, is christened by her namesake's granddaughter, Queen Elizabeth II.[46]
The nuclear sub USS San Francisco collides at full speed with an undersea mountain south of Guam. One man is killed, but the sub surfaces and is repaired.[47]
A 6.1-magnitude earthquake in northern Costa Rica kills 15 people and injures 32.[48]

Gunmen from an offshoot of the Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda attack a bus carrying the Togo national football team on its way to the 2010 Africa Cup of Nations, killing three people and injuring another nine.[49]

enAnchors.text: Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda

D0108.en: Sitting US Congresswoman Gabby Giffords is shot in the head along with 18 others in a mass shooting in Tucson, Arizona. Giffords survived the assassination attempt, but six others died, including John Roll, a federal judge.[50]
Joaquín Guzmán, widely regarded as the world's most powerful drug trafficker, is recaptured following his escape from a maximum security prison in Mexico.[51]
West Air Sweden Flight 294 crashes near the Swedish reservoir of Akkajaure; both pilots, the only people on board, are killed.[52]

Ukraine International Airlines Flight 752 crashes immediately after takeoff at Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport; all 176 on board are killed. The plane was shot down by an Iranian anti-aircraft missile.[53]
Twenty-three people are killed in what is described as a police ″massacre″ in La Vega, Caracas, Venezuela.[54]
Supporters of former Brazil president Jair Bolsonaro storm the Brazilian Congress.[55]

D0109.en: Twelfth Council of Toledo: King Erwig of the Visigoths initiates a council in which he implements diverse measures against the Jews in Spain.[1]
An earthquake in Dingxiang, China kills an estimated 32,300.[2]

Jin–Song Wars: Invading Jurchen soldiers from the Jin dynasty besiege and sack Bianjing (Kaifeng), the capital of the Song dynasty of China, and abduct Emperor Qinzong of Song and others, ending the Northern Song period.[3]
The Jewish population of Basel, believed by the residents to be the cause of the ongoing Black Death, is rounded up and incinerated.[4]
The trial of Joan of Arc begins in Rouen.[5]
Sicily earthquake: The first of two earthquakes destroys parts of Sicily and Malta. After the second quake on 11 January, the death toll is estimated at between 60,000 and 100,000 people.[6]
Ahmad Shah Durrani defeats the Marathas in the Battle of Barari Ghat.[7]

The nationally known image of the Black Nazarene in the Philippines was transferred from what is now Rizal Park to its present shrine in the minor basilica of Quiapo Church. This is annually commemorated through its Traslación (solemn transfer) in the streets of Manila and is attended by millions of devotees.[8][9][10]
Connecticut becomes the fifth state to ratify the United States Constitution.[11]
Treaty of Jassy between Russian and Ottoman Empire is signed, ending the Russo-Turkish War of 1787–92.[12]
Jean-Pierre Blanchard becomes the first person to fly in a balloon in the United States.[13]
British Prime Minister William Pitt the Younger introduces an income tax of two shillings to the pound to raise funds for Great Britain's war effort in the Napoleonic Wars.[14]

enAnchors.text: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

D0109.en: Admiral Horatio Lord Nelson receives a state funeral and is interred in St Paul's Cathedral.[15]
Humphry Davy tests his safety lamp for miners at Hebburn Colliery.[16]
The Portuguese prince Pedro I of Brazil decides to stay in Brazil against the orders of the Portuguese King João VI, beginning the Brazilian independence process.[17]
The French Academy of Sciences announces the Daguerreotype photography process.[18]
The 7.9 Mw  Fort Tejon earthquake shakes Central and Southern California with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent).[19]
British forces finally defeat Rajab Ali Khan of Chittagong[20]
American Civil War: "Star of the West" incident occurs near Charleston, South Carolina.[21]
Mississippi becomes the second state to secede from the Union before the outbreak of the American Civil War.[22]

enAnchors.text: Mississippi in the American Civil War

D0109.en: Umberto I becomes King of Italy.[23]
Hallam Tennyson, 2nd Baron Tennyson, son of the poet Alfred Tennyson, becomes the second Governor-General of Australia.[24]
Ernest Shackleton, leading the Nimrod Expedition to the South Pole, plants the British flag 97 nautical miles (180 km; 112 mi) from the South Pole, the farthest anyone had ever reached at that time.
The Phi Beta Sigma fraternity is founded by African-American students at Howard University in Washington D.C., United States.[25]
World War I: The Battle of Gallipoli concludes with an Ottoman Empire victory when the last Allied forces are evacuated from the peninsula.[26]
World War I: The Battle of Rafa is fought near the Egyptian border with Palestine.[27]
Battle of Bear Valley: The last battle of the American Indian Wars.[28]
Ukrainian War of Independence: The All-Ukrainian Central Executive Committee outlaws the Makhnovshchina by decree, igniting the Bolshevik–Makhnovist conflict.[29]
Greco-Turkish War: The First Battle of İnönü, the first battle of the war, begins near Eskişehir in Anatolia.[30]
Juan de la Cierva makes the first autogyro flight.[31]
Lithuanian residents of the Memel Territory rebel against the League of Nations' decision to leave the area as a mandated region under French control.
A fire at the Laurier Palace movie theatre in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, kills 78 children.[32]
World War II: First flight of the Avro Lancaster.[33]
World War II: The Sixth United States Army begins the invasion of Lingayen Gulf.[34]
British Prime Minister Sir Anthony Eden resigns from office following his failure to retake the Suez Canal from Egyptian sovereignty.[35]

enAnchors.text: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

D0109.en: The Vega de Tera dam fails, triggering a disastrous flood that nearly destroys the town of Ribadelago and kills 144 residents.[36]
President of Egypt Gamal Abdel Nasser opens construction on the Aswan Dam by detonating ten tons of dynamite to demolish twenty tons of granite on the east bank of the Nile.[37]
British authorities announce they have uncovered the Soviet Portland spy ring in London.
Apollo program: NASA announces plans to build the C-5 rocket launch vehicle, then known as the "Advanced Saturn", to carry human beings to the Moon.[38]
Martyrs' Day: Several Panamanian youths try to raise the Panamanian flag in the U.S.-controlled Panama Canal Zone, leading to fighting between U.S. military and Panamanian civilians.
Representatives from the United States and Iraq meet at the Geneva Peace Conference to try to find a peaceful resolution to the Iraqi invasion of Kuwait.[39]
The Assembly of the Serb People in Bosnia and Herzegovina proclaims the creation of Republika Srpska, a new state within Yugoslavia.

enAnchors.text: Assembly of the Serb People in Bosnia and Herzegovina

D0109.en: The first discoveries of extrasolar planets are announced by astronomers Aleksander Wolszczan and Dale Frail. They discovered two planets orbiting the pulsar PSR 1257+12.[40]

First Chechen War: Chechen separatists launch a raid against the helicopter airfield and later a civilian hospital in the city of Kizlyar in the neighboring Dagestan, which turns into a massive hostage crisis involving thousands of civilians.
Comair Flight 3272 crashes in Raisinville Township in Monroe County, Michigan, killing 29 people.[41]
TANS Perú Flight 222 crashes on approach to Chachapoyas Airport in Chachapoyas, Peru, killing 46 people.[42]

An inflatable boat carrying illegal Albanian emigrants stalls near the Karaburun Peninsula en route to Brindisi, Italy; exposure to the elements kills 28. This is the second deadliest marine disaster in Albanian history.[43]
Mahmoud Abbas wins the election to succeed Yasser Arafat as President of the Palestinian National Authority, replacing interim president Rawhi Fattouh.[44]
The Sudan People's Liberation Movement and the Government of Sudan sign the Comprehensive Peace Agreement to end the Second Sudanese Civil War.[45]
Apple CEO Steve Jobs introduces the original iPhone at a Macworld keynote in San Francisco.[46]
Iran Air Flight 277 crashes near Urmia in the northwest of the country, in icy conditions, killing 78 people.[47]
An explosion at a Mitsubishi Materials chemical plant in Yokkaichi, Japan, kills at least five people and injures 17 others.[48]

The perpetrators of the Charlie Hebdo shooting in Paris two days earlier are both killed after a hostage situation; a second hostage situation, related to the Charlie Hebdo shooting, occurs at a Jewish market in Vincennes.[49]
A mass poisoning at a funeral in Mozambique involving beer that was contaminated with Burkholderia gladioli leaves 75 dead and over 230 people ill.[50]
Sriwijaya Air Flight 182 crashes north of Jakarta, Indonesia, killing all 62 people on board.[51]

D0110.en: The Western Han dynasty ends when Wang Mang claims that the divine Mandate of Heaven called for the end of the dynasty and the beginning of his own, the Xin dynasty.[2]
Lucius Calpurnius Piso Licinianus is appointed by Galba as deputy Roman Emperor.[3]
Pope Fabian succeeds Anterus to become the twentieth pope of Rome.[4]
Robert Guiscard conquers Palermo in Sicily for the Normans.[5]
Philip the Good, the Duke of Burgundy, establishes the Order of the Golden Fleece, the most prestigious, exclusive, and expensive order of chivalry in the world.[6]
Stephen III of Moldavia defeats the Ottoman Empire at the Battle of Vaslui.[7]
Archbishop William Laud is beheaded for treason at the Tower of London.[8]
American Revolution: Thomas Paine publishes his pamphlet Common Sense.[9]
The Siege of Dunlap's Station begins near Cincinnati during the Northwest Indian War.[10]
The first steamboat on the Ohio River or the Mississippi River arrives in New Orleans, 82 days after departing from Pittsburgh.[11]
American Civil War: Florida becomes the third state to secede from the Union.[12]
The Metropolitan Railway, the world's oldest underground railway, opens between Paddington and Farringdon, marking the beginning of the London Underground.[13]

enAnchors.text: Paddington tube station (Circle and Hammersmith & City lines)

D0110.en: John D. Rockefeller incorporates Standard Oil.[14]
The Plan of Tuxtepec is announced.[15]
The first great Texas oil gusher is discovered at Spindletop in Beaumont, Texas.[16]
New York: Automobile Club of America installs signs on major highways.[17]
World War I: Imperial Russia begins the Erzurum Offensive, leading to the defeat of the Ottoman Empire's Third Army.[18]
Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition: Seven survivors of the Ross Sea party were rescued after being stranded for several months.[19]
The Treaty of Versailles takes effect, officially ending World War I for all combatant nations except the United States.[20]
League of Nations Covenant automatically enters into force after the Treaty of Versailles is ratified by Germany.[21]
Fritz Lang's futuristic film Metropolis is released in Germany.[22]
World War II: The Greek army captures Kleisoura.[23]
The first General Assembly of the United Nations assembles in the Methodist Central Hall, Westminster. Fifty-one nations are represented.[24]
The United States Army Signal Corps successfully conducts Project Diana, bouncing radio waves off the Moon and receiving the reflected signals.[25]
BOAC Flight 781, a de Havilland DH.106 Comet 1, explodes and falls into the Tyrrhenian Sea, killing 35 people.[26]
Tashkent Declaration, a peace agreement between India and Pakistan signed that resolved the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965.
Sheikh Mujibur Rahman returns to the newly independent Bangladesh as president after spending over nine months in prison in Pakistan.
The New England Journal of Medicine publishes the letter Addiction Rare in Patients Treated with Narcotics, which is later misused to downplay the general risk of addiction to opioids.[27][28]

enAnchors.text: The New England Journal of Medicine
Addiction Rare in Patients Treated with Narcotics

D0110.en: Salvadoran Civil War: The FMLN launches its first major offensive, gaining control of most of Morazán and Chalatenango departments

Holy See–United States relations: The United States and Holy See (Vatican City) re-establish full diplomatic relations after almost 117 years, overturning the United States Congress's 1867 ban on public funding for such a diplomatic envoy.

Sandinista Daniel Ortega becomes president of Nicaragua and vows to continue the transformation to socialism and alliance with the Soviet Union and Cuba; American policy continues to support the Contras in their revolt against the Nicaraguan government.
Time Warner is formed by the merger of Time Inc. and Warner Communications.
Crossair Flight 498, a Saab 340 aircraft, crashes in Niederhasli, Switzerland, after taking off from Zurich Airport, killing 13 people.[29]
A general strike begins in Guinea in an attempt to get President Lansana Conté to resign.[32]
A bombing at Jamrud in Pakistan, kills at least 30 people and injures 78 others.[33]
More than 100 people are killed and 270 injured in several bomb blasts in the Quetta area of Pakistan.[34]

A traffic accident between an oil tanker truck and passenger coach en route to Shikarpur from Karachi on the Pakistan National Highway Link Road near Gulshan-e-Hadeed, Karachi, killing at least 62 people.[35]
A 13-year-old American girl, Jayme Closs, is found alive in Gordon, Wisconsin, having been kidnapped 88 days earlier from her parents' home whilst they were murdered.[36]

D0111.en: Nika riots in Constantinople: A quarrel between supporters of different chariot teams—the Blues and the Greens—in the Hippodrome escalates into violence.[1]
Conquest of Mecca: Muhammad and his followers conquer the city, and the Quraysh association of clans surrenders.[2]
Sack of Mecca by the Qarmatians.[3]
Theodora is crowned empress of the Byzantine Empire.[4]
Vladislaus II, Duke of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia.[5]
First recorded lottery in England.[6]
Arauco War: A Spanish army is defeated by local Mapuche-Huilliches as it tries to cross Bueno River in Southern Chile.[7]

The first American life insurance company, the Corporation for Relief of Poor and Distressed Presbyterian Ministers and of the Poor and Distressed Widows and Children of the Presbyterian Ministers (now part of Unum Group), is incorporated in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.[8]
William Herschel discovers Titania and Oberon, two moons of Uranus.[9]

enAnchors.text: Timeline of discovery of Solar System planets and their moons

D0111.en: The Michigan Territory is created.[10]
The Great Savannah Fire of 1820 destroys over 400 buildings in Savannah, Georgia.[11]
Taiping Rebellion: Hong Xiuquan proclaims the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom, starting the Jintian Uprising.[12][13]
American Civil War: Alabama secedes from the United States.[14]

American Civil War: The three-day Battle of Arkansas Post concludes as General John McClernand and Admiral David Dixon Porter capture Fort Hindman and secure control over the Arkansas River for the Union.[15][16]
American Civil War: CSS Alabama encounters and sinks the USS Hatteras off Galveston Lighthouse in Texas.[16]
The Anglo-Zulu War begins.[17]
Grand Canyon National Monument is created.
Immigrant textile workers in Lawrence, Massachusetts, go on strike when wages are reduced in response to a mandated shortening of the work week.[18]
The Karluk, flagship of the Canadian Arctic Expedition, sinks after being crushed by ice.[19]
The Kingsland munitions factory explosion occurs as a result of sabotage.
Leonard Thompson becomes the first person to be injected with insulin.
Occupation of the Ruhr: Troops from France and Belgium occupy the Ruhr area to force Germany to make its World War I reparation payments.
Louis B. Mayer, head of film studio Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer (MGM), announces the creation of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences, at a banquet in Los Angeles, California.[20]
Amelia Earhart becomes the first person to fly solo from Hawaii to California.
World War II: Japanese forces capture Kuala Lumpur, the capital of the Federated Malay States.
World War II: Japanese forces attack Tarakan in Borneo, Netherlands Indies (Battle of Tarakan)
The Republic of China agrees to the Sino-British New Equal Treaty and the Sino-American New Equal Treaty.

enAnchors.text: Sino-British Treaty for the Relinquishment of Extra-Territorial Rights in China

D0111.en: Italian-American anarchist Carlo Tresca is assassinated in New York City.
Enver Hoxha, Secretary General of the Communist Party of Albania, declares the People's Republic of Albania with himself as head of state.
The first "networked" television broadcasts took place as KDKA-TV in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania goes on the air connecting the east coast and mid-west programming.
The African Convention is founded in Dakar, Senegal.
36 people are killed when Lufthansa Flight 502 crashes on approach to Rio de Janeiro/Galeão International Airport in Brazil.[21]
Throgs Neck Bridge over the East River, linking New York City's boroughs of The Bronx and Queens, opens to road traffic.
Cold War: While tied to its pier in Polyarny, the Soviet submarine B-37 is destroyed when fire breaks out in its torpedo compartment.
An avalanche on Huascarán in Peru causes around 4,000 deaths.[22]

Surgeon General of the United States Dr. Luther Terry, M.D., publishes the landmark report Smoking and Health: Report of the Advisory Committee to the Surgeon General of the Public Health Service saying that smoking may be hazardous to health, sparking national and worldwide anti-smoking efforts.

enAnchors.text: Surgeon General of the United States

D0111.en: The Tbilisi Metro is opened.[23]
East Pakistan renames itself Bangladesh.
Major League Baseball owners vote in approval of the American League adopting the designated hitter position.
United Airlines Flight 2885 crashes after takeoff from Detroit Metropolitan Airport, killing three.[24]
The Gateway Bridge, Brisbane, Queensland, Australia is officially opened.
The Irish Government announces the end of a 15-year broadcasting ban on the IRA and its political arm Sinn Féin.
51 people are killed in a plane crash in María La Baja, Colombia.[25]
Space Shuttle program: STS-72 launches from the Kennedy Space Center marking the start of the 74th Space Shuttle mission and the 10th flight of Endeavour.[26]
Over 100 people are killed in the Sidi-Hamed massacre in Algeria.[27]
Illinois Governor George Ryan commutes the death sentences of 167 prisoners on Illinois's death row based on the Jon Burge scandal.
One French soldier and 17 militants are killed in a failed attempt to free a French hostage in Bulo Marer, Somalia.
COVID-19 pandemic in Hubei: Municipal health officials in Wuhan announce the first recorded death from COVID-19.[28]

D0112.en: Byzantine Emperor Zeno is forced to flee his capital at Constantinople, and his general, Basiliscus gains control of the empire.[1]
Gustav I of Sweden is crowned King of Sweden, having already reigned since his election in June 1523.[2]
Bayinnaung, who would go on to assemble the largest empire in the history of Southeast Asia, is crowned King of Burma.[3]
The city of Belém, Brazil is founded on the Amazon River delta by Portuguese captain Francisco Caldeira Castelo Branco.[4]
Federalist Thomas Pinckney appointed first U.S. minister to Britain.[5]

John Rennie's scheme to defend St Mary's Church, Reculver, founded in 669, from coastal erosion is abandoned in favour of demolition, despite the church being an exemplar of Anglo-Saxon architecture and sculpture.[6]
The organizational meeting leading to the creation of the Wernerian Natural History Society, a former Scottish learned society, is held in Edinburgh.[7]
The Palermo rising takes place in Sicily against the Bourbon Kingdom of the Two Sicilies.[8]
The Royal Aeronautical Society is formed in London.[9]
Yohannes IV is crowned Emperor of Ethiopia in Axum, the first imperial coronation in that city in over 200 years.[10]
The National Trust is founded in the United Kingdom.[11]

enAnchors.text: National Trust for Places of Historic Interest or Natural Beauty

D0112.en: The University of the Philippines College of Law is established.[12]

enAnchors.text: University of the Philippines College of Law

D0112.en: The United States House of Representatives rejects a proposed constitutional amendment to require states to give women the right to vote.[13]

Oswald Boelcke and Max Immelmann become the first German aviators to earn the Pour le Mérite, receive the German Empire's highest military award, for achieving eight aerial victories each over Allied aircraft.[14]
The Minnie Pit Disaster coal mining accident occurs in Halmer End, Staffordshire, in which 155 men and boys die.[15]
Hattie Caraway becomes the first woman elected to the United States Senate.[16]
Casas Viejas incident: 22 peasants killed by the Security and Assault Corps in Casas Viejas, Spain.[17]
World War II: United States President Franklin D. Roosevelt creates the National War Labor Board.[18]
World War II: The Red Army begins the Vistula–Oder Offensive.[19]
A Martin 2-0-2 and Douglas DC-3 collide over Boone County, Kentucky, killing 15 people.[20]
Vietnam War: Operation Chopper, the first American combat mission and first American helicopter assault in the war, takes place.[21]
Rebels in Zanzibar begin a revolt known as the Zanzibar Revolution and proclaim a republic.[22]
Lyndon B. Johnson states that the United States should stay in South Vietnam until Communist aggression there is ended.[23]
Dr. James Bedford becomes the first person to be cryonically preserved with intent of future resuscitation.

The New York Jets of the American Football League defeat the Baltimore Colts of the National Football League to win Super Bowl III in what is considered to be one of the greatest upsets in sports history.
Biafra capitulates, ending the Nigerian Civil War.

The Harrisburg Seven: Rev. Philip Berrigan and five other activists are indicted on charges of conspiring to kidnap Henry Kissinger and of plotting to blow up the heating tunnels of federal buildings in Washington, D.C.
The United Nations Security Council votes 11–1 to allow the Palestine Liberation Organization to participate in a Security Council debate (without voting rights).
Space Shuttle program: Congressman and future NASA Administrator Bill Nelson lifts off from Kennedy Space Center aboard Columbia on mission STS-61-C as a payload specialist.
A seven-day pogrom breaks out against the Armenian civilian population of Baku, Azerbaijan, during which Armenians were beaten, tortured, murdered, and expelled from the city.
Persian Gulf War: An act of the U.S. Congress authorizes the use of American military force to drive Iraq out of Kuwait.

Space Shuttle program: Atlantis launches from the Kennedy Space Center on mission STS-81 to the Russian space station Mir, carrying astronaut Jerry M. Linenger for a four-month stay on board the station, replacing astronaut John E. Blaha.[24]
Nineteen European nations agree to forbid human cloning.
Downtown Disney opens to the public as part of the Disneyland Resort in Anaheim, California.
The world's largest ocean liner, RMS Queen Mary 2, makes its maiden voyage.
Deep Impact launches from Cape Canaveral on a Delta II rocket.[25]
A stampede during the Stoning of the Devil ritual on the last day at the Hajj in Mina, Saudi Arabia, kills at least 362 Muslim pilgrims.
Comet C/2006 P1 (McNaught), one of the brightest comets ever observed is at its zenith visible during the day.
An earthquake in Haiti occurs, killing between 220,000 and 300,000 people and destroying much of the capital Port-au-Prince.[26]

Violent protests occur in Bucharest, Romania, as two-day-old demonstrations continue against President Traian Băsescu's economic austerity measures. Clashes are reported in numerous Romanian cities between protesters and law enforcement officers.
Government raids kill 143 Boko Haram fighters in Kolofata, Cameroon.
Ten people are killed and 15 wounded in a bombing near the Blue Mosque in Istanbul.
Taal Volcano in the Philippines erupts and kills 39 people.

D0113.en: The Nika riots break out, during the racing season at the Hippodrome in Constantinople, as a result of discontent with the rule of the Emperor Justinian I.[2][3]
Sicut Dudum, forbidding the enslavement by the Spanish of the Guanche natives in Canary Islands who had converted, or were converting to, Christianity, is promulgated by Pope Eugene IV.
Henry Howard, Earl of Surrey, is sentenced to death for treason, on the grounds of having quartered his arms to make them similar to those of the King, Henry VIII of England.[4]
Nicolas Jean Hugon de Bassville, representative of Revolutionary France, is lynched by a mob in Rome.

French Revolutionary Wars: A naval battle between a French ship of the line and two British frigates off the coast of Brittany ends with the French vessel running aground, resulting in over 900 deaths.
War of 1812: British troops capture Fort Peter in St. Marys, Georgia, the only battle of the war to take place in the state.
The design of the Greek flag is adopted by the First National Assembly at Epidaurus.
United States President Andrew Jackson writes to Vice President elect Martin Van Buren expressing his opposition to South Carolina's defiance of federal authority in the Nullification Crisis.

enAnchors.text: Vice President of the United States

D0113.en: The steamship Lexington burns and sinks four miles off the coast of Long Island with the loss of 139 lives.

Dr. William Brydon, an assistant surgeon in the British East India Company Army during the First Anglo-Afghan War, becomes famous for being the sole survivor of an army of 4,500 men and 12,000 camp followers when he reaches the safety of a garrison in Jalalabad, Afghanistan.
The Treaty of Cahuenga ends the Mexican–American War in California.
Establishment of the Colony of Vancouver Island.
